reviews.author: Rizwan Ghafoor
reviews.text: This beautiful place is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. It’s a full-day excursion tour with many magnificent caves to explore. The breathtaking scenes are accompanied by various activities to enjoy. It’s a must-visit destination for anyone looking to add a touch of adventure to their bucket list.

reviews.author: Kirk Chantraine
reviews.text: Brilliant, beautiful area to explore. Highly recommend the tour from Emeraude (see my review there for details). Stunning landscapes - absolutely incredible.

reviews.author: Liviu Apostol-Ionescu
reviews.text: Very nice place. A must see when you come to Vietnam. I recommend the minimum 2 nights stay as most of the people only take one night and don’t get to see the most beautiful places. In the first day you only get to see the most crowded places. In the next day we went to a remote location and enjoyed some nice views and activities.

reviews.author: greta kaden
reviews.text: Beautiful but only with the right light and clear blue sky / sunshine. Not that special when the weather isn’t perfect. Also VERY touristy and overcrowded… the cave is really special and huge though. Mixed feelings.

reviews.author: Viera V
reviews.text: A must do when in Vietnam 🇻🇳 Short trip from Hanoi, about 2hrs 30m. If you book a tour with transit, you will get picked up from your hotel and they have stop over on the way to the terminal. We spent a weekend on the cruise, which was sufficient. There was a welcome drink, buffet lunch, dinner, breakfast, entertainment. You admire beautiful views from your room balcony and get to do trips to the caves, kayaking, swimming. Super enjoyable

reviews.author: Common ಜೀವನ
reviews.text: Wow! What a place!😍 You must add it to your Vietnam itinerary. The cruise, cave exploration, view & the food, all were amazing! Here you can even enjoy few activities like Kayaking, Bamboo boat & speed boat.

reviews.author: Nirav Narola
reviews.text: Ha Long Bay, a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in northern Vietnam, is one of the country's most iconic natural wonders and a must-visit destination for any traveler. I recently had the opportunity to explore this breathtaking bay, and I can confidently say it captivated my heart with its stunning landscapes and rich cultural experiences. The first thing that strikes you about Ha Long Bay is its remarkable beauty. The bay is dotted with over 1,600 limestone islands and islets, many of which are covered in lush greenery. The sight of these towering karsts rising dramatically from the emerald waters is simply awe-inspiring. Whether viewed from a boat, a kayak, or the shore, the scenery is breathtaking and offers numerous photographic opportunities. A visit to Ha Long Bay is often best experienced through a cruise. There are various options available, from day trips to overnight excursions on luxury junks. I opted for an overnight cruise, and it was one of the highlights of my trip. The crew was incredibly attentive, and the accommodation was comfortable, creating the perfect ambiance for relaxation. The daily itinerary included visits to hidden caves, swimming in the bay, and enjoying sumptuous meals featuring fresh seafood while watching the sunset over the water. Apart from cruise activities, Ha Long Bay offers plenty of opportunities for adventure and exploration. Kayaking through the bay’s smaller lagoons and caves was a thrilling experience that allowed me to get closer to the stunning rock formations.

reviews.author: Ane Jørgensen
reviews.text: Must see in Vietnam- despite it is so turisted. Were on a luxury cruise which was a great experience in it self - but I assume all boats give the same tours and the nature is stunning.

reviews.author: Rasputin
reviews.text: Ha Long Bay, what an awesome and beautiful place. I booked one day trip from Hanoi. But I think I‘ve seen a more crowded place. The number of boats and people on “the beach” and in the cave is too much. With all that being said, I would not miss this place if I was in Vietnam 🇻🇳 They have 2-3 days cruises which will be more appropriate because early in the day the day cruise people are not there.’

reviews.author: Cindy Shelberg
reviews.text: We booked a LaCosta Cruise for a day tour. Our cruise director Duk Tom was very informative, fun and made our trip quite enjoyable. The scenery is stunning. The caves were outstanding and the speedboat tour is a must. To top it off we were able to see the sunset on the water. Note, there were crowds and lots of boats but tour and sightseeing was well managed. Highly recommend a visit.

reviews.author: om tiwari
reviews.text: I recently visited Ha Long Bay, and it was an unforgettable experience. The sheer beauty of the limestone karsts rising dramatically from the emerald green waters is absolutely mesmerizing. The scenery feels like something out of a dream. I opted for a day cruise, which allowed us to explore the caves, enjoy kayaking, and relax while taking in the serene surroundings. Sung Sot Cave was an incredible highlight with its impressive stalactites and stalagmites. The boat ride was comfortable, and the staff were friendly and accommodating, making the entire experience even more enjoyable. If you have more time, I highly recommend the overnight cruises, which I plan to do next time. One small downside was the number of tourists in certain areas, but it didn’t take away from the overall beauty and tranquility of the place. The local food served on the boat was fresh and flavorful, adding an authentic Vietnamese touch to the trip. If you’re visiting Vietnam, Ha Long Bay is a destination you simply cannot miss. It’s a UNESCO World Heritage Site for a reason, and it truly lives up to the hype!

reviews.author: anjali sahu
reviews.text: Ha Long Bay is absolutely breathtaking! The limestone karsts rising out of the emerald-green waters create a surreal, otherworldly landscape. Taking a boat cruise through the bay was a highlight, with stunning views at every turn. We also visited hidden caves and floating villages, which added to the experience. The serenity and natural beauty of the area make it a must-visit for anyone traveling to Vietnam. Highly recommend spending at least a day or two to truly soak in the beauty of this UNESCO World Heritage site!

reviews.author: J P
reviews.text: Superb scenery. Tourist attractions packed but was to be expected. Stay a night and take in the amazing surroundings first thing in the morning. Choose your cruise carefully!

reviews.author: Divakshi Bhardwaj
reviews.text: Discovering one of the country’s most iconic and picturesque natural wonders. Just a few hours from Hanoi, you’re greeted by a breathtaking landscape of limestone karsts rising from serene waters. We spent the day drifting through this tranquil paradise, taking in the mesmerizing views.😍✨ Must visit this place for a surreal experience with your friends & family!

reviews.author: Hezil dsouza
reviews.text: Ha Long Bay is indeed one of Vietnam's most iconic and popular tourist destinations, attracting travelers from all around the world. With its stunning view from different cave and emerald waters, it's no wonder it draws such a large crowds. Lots of fun activities like kayaking, walking up the stairs to see sun song cave, Tip top island, beach etc. lots of boat sailing on the ha long bay island.

reviews.author: Cees Embrechts
reviews.text: If you like mass tourism and crowds, this is definitely your gig! You are welcomed on one of the hundreds of tourist boats. Dropped off at one of the artificial islands and popular caves with hundreds of like minded people. You will love it. From your sundeck you can witness a wide variety of cruise ships or if you are lucky you might catch a glimpse of the sunset.

reviews.author: Jessie Ong
reviews.text: Ha long bay, a beautiful place that one should not missed if you are in Hanoi. We took a day tour package, and the driver picked us from our hotel in downtown. It was around 2-3 hours to ha long, and just nice to board the cruise for buffet lunch. Then we went on to some cave tour and kayaking. We enjoyed the sunset from the cruise and the cruise offered buy one free one happy hour drink 🍷 have a sip of cocktail while enjoying the sunset on cruise, perfect 🤩

reviews.author: carol barreta
reviews.text: The location definitely worth the visit once is amazing the view and the energy of the place is nice. However, I had the feeling that seems too touristic and sort of lost the magic. I decided to spend the night but I wouldn’t recommend. Of course see the sun born in the next morning was an unique experience, but just spend I think works pretty fine. You can find many different types of boats: party, chill, family, and so on, which makes everyone a good target to make the visit.

reviews.author: Tulsi mahto
reviews.text: Tourist attractions- world unesco site- can book over night cruise- day visit is not enough to explore beauty of Ha long bay. Try book 5 star cruise have decent food . They cover all visit plan in ha long bay . Must do …..

reviews.author: Ashan Mudannayake
reviews.text: This place was simply breathtaking. It was my first overnight cruise experience, and it was totally worth it. The scenes were exceptional wherever you looked, and the amazing company of the Vietnamese people made it even better.

reviews.author: Alok Aditya
reviews.text: This UNESCO world heritage site is a must visit place in Vietnam. You can take day cruise as well as overnight cruises to visit this area. Atleast 2D1N cruise is recommended to be able to visit the different spots and enjoy the tour. There are four different routes which cruises usually take. You can choose your route basis spots that you want to visit and crowd that you want to encounter! Do visit Sang Sot cave and Ti-top island. Also you can connect with cruises directly via email to get better prices.

reviews.author: florent magnoac
reviews.text: Splendid. Breathtaking. Marvellous. A true wonder that is not to be missed. I totally recomment the 2 days/ 1 night cruise at least to fully enjoy the setting. Paid 128$ for the cruise everything included except drinks...and you get a lot of food. You get to visit a cave, go to a small island beach and viewpoint. Learn to cook spring rolls. Fish in the sea. And in the morning you will visit a pearl farm and enjoy kayaking in the bay. A mesmerizing experience.

reviews.author: Arjun Sehgal
reviews.text: A must visit place in Vietnam. Not very far from Hanoi. Hundreds of small islands, caves and lagoons. Beautiful beaches. The downside is too many tourists and long lines. Carry a water flask as plastic is not allowed here.

reviews.author: Daniel
reviews.text: I did the tour twice now. One tour back in 2017 and one tour in 2024. the tour is still breathtaking and you can’t get enough from the view. Make sure to choose a tour that fits your interest since there are almost unlimited options and routes that you can take. My opinion is that you have to stay at least 2D1N otherwise it’s not worth the long ride.

reviews.author: Quỳnh Dương
reviews.text: I had 3 days trip on a small cruise and it was so nice. The staffs were friendly and took good care of customers. They brought us some extra deserts. That was so cute. The view was amazing too. Defo should try HaLong bay cruise once!

reviews.author: NIDHISH GUPTA
reviews.text: Halong Bay is a breathtaking natural wonder and a UNESCO World Heritage Site in northern Vietnam. Known for its emerald waters and thousands of limestone karsts and islets, it offers a surreal seascape that feels like stepping into a dream. Activities like cruising, kayaking, and exploring hidden caves or floating villages make the experience unforgettable. The sunsets and sunrises over the bay are particularly magical. Whether you're seeking relaxation or adventure, Halong Bay’s serene beauty and unique charm make it a must-visit destination for any traveler.

reviews.author: Julayn Adams
reviews.text: A must do when coming to Hanoi. The caves were really busy but well worth the wait in line. I’d also recommend hiking the Ti Top mountain for a great view, if able. We chose kayaking over the bamboo boats, great choice!

reviews.author: Anchal Garg
reviews.text: Just few hours away from Hanoi, this place is a must visit in Vietnam 🇻🇳 It’s so damm beautiful and picturesque! Discovering this beauty was the best thing of the trip ♥️ Also it’s part of the World heritage site, so definitely 💯 recommended and worth it!

reviews.author: Dino Singh
reviews.text: Ha Long Bay's ethereal beauty is unrivaled, with its emerald waters and towering limestone karsts creating a dreamlike landscape. Exploring hidden caves and floating villages reveals a rich cultural tapestry. The bay’s serene ambiance at dawn is enchanting, making it a must-visit for nature lovers and tranquility seekers.

reviews.author: Linda Fox
reviews.text: Ha Long Bay is absolutely breathtaking – a truly unique experience! The dramatic scenery is so cinematic, you can almost picture King Kong emerging from the sea (fun fact: parts of Kong: Skull Island were filmed here). We sailed on Paradise Elegance 2, and the experience was flawless. The staff were outstanding, with a special mention to Korina from the Philippines, who took excellent care of us. The rooms were beautifully designed, with a super comfortable bed, a spacious bathroom (impressive for a boat), and the added luxury of a private balcony. The food was exquisite, and the entertainment was top-notch. Highly recommend this unforgettable adventure!
